response:
to find the denominator we must store the count of prime factors in our mental store.
***
the number one hundred factorial contains many factors of five that we need to store.
***
we calculate the exponent of five by storing the sum of integer divisions of one hundred.
***
the total count of five factors is twenty-four which we must store carefully.
***
the power of ten in the divisor is fifty so we store this value for comparison.
***
since there are only twenty-four fives we store the result that the fraction simplifies.
***
the remaining factors of two will be stored in the numerator after cancellation.
***
the denominator will be a power of two that we must store as the final answer.
***
we store the value of two raised to the twenty-sixth power as the result.
***
the final answer is stored as two to the power of twenty-six in our store.
